steps.title: Identify the tense requirement
steps.content: The sentence uses a "when" time clause paired with the main clause "she became the state's first female chief executive." "Became" is simple past, anchored to the specific date November 5, 2003. The verb in the "when" clause must match that past-tense anchor.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'begins': simple present tense; describes an ongoing or habitual action, which is inconsistent with the fixed past date in the sentence. ✗
  • Choice B — 'will begin': future tense; describes something yet to happen, which contradicts the past date of November 5, 2003. ✗
  • Choice C — 'began': simple past tense; matches "became" and correctly places the action at the same past moment on that specific date. ✓
  • Choice D — 'has begun': present perfect tense; describes an action with relevance to the present, not one completed at a specific past date. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
